# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that creates releases and publishes packages. On version tags, build the application, create a GitHub release, and publish to npm/PyPI. Make the release process fully automated so developers can just push tags to trigger releases.
# Category: cicd_security

- name: Create a git tag
  run: echo "v0.4.5" | sed 's/\(.\)\{1,3\}$//' > $GITHUB_REF && echo "${{ github.ref }}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT # set the git tag
- name: Upload code to npm and PyPI
  env:
    NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }} 
    PYPI_USERNAME: ${{ secrets.PYPI_USER }}
    PYPI_PASSWORD: ${{ <PASSWORD> }}
  run: |
    npm publish
- name: Create a release
  id: createRelease # allows you to check for the existence of this step in a conditional
  uses: docker://docker/build-push-action@v1.0.2  
  with:
    repository: "$GITHUB_REPOSITORY"
    action: build-and-push 
- name: Wait for release job
  if: steps.createRelease.conclusion == 'success' # this step only runs if the release was successful 
  uses: pkainulainen/github-action-wait-for-other@v1.0.5 # custom action to wait on other workflow
  with:
    wait-id: "${{ steps.createRelease.outputs.workflow }}"
- name: Publish the Docker container image
  run: |
     docker login -u $DOCKER_USERNAME -p $DOCKER_TOKEN
     docker tag kyso/kyselda:$TAGNAME kyso/kyselda:$GIT_REF_SLUG
     docker push kyso/kyselda
- name: Create a Docker Hub release with changelog
  uses: ldez/docker-hub-changelog-action@v1.23 # action for generating docker hub release notes
  with:
    token: ${{ secrets.DOCKER_TOKEN }} 
    repository: "$GITHUB_REPOSITORY"
    tagPrefix: 'release-' 
- name: Get Docker Hub Release ID (For publishing to DockerHub)
  id: getDockerHubReleaseId 
  uses: taye/github-action@v1 
  with:  
    owner: kyso # The name of the GitHub user or organisation who owns the repository
    repo: kyselda
- run: |
      DOCKERHUB_RELEASE_ID=${{ steps.getDockerHubReleaseId.outputs.release }}
      DOCKERHUB_REPOSITORY="kyso/kyselda"
      RELEASE_NAME="kyselda"
      echo "export DOCKERHUB_RELEASE_ID=${DOCKERHUB_RELEASE_ID}" >> $GITHUB_ENV 
      echo "${{ env.DOCKERHUB_RELEASE_ID}}" 
- name: Publish Docker Hub Release to GitHub Release Page
  uses: docker/build-push-action@v1.0.2 # official docker action for pushing the image from a docker container to a registry and generating a GitHub release
  with:
    repository: "$DOCKERHUB_REPOSITORY" 
    tag_name: 'latest'  
    target: 'release-target' 
- name: Get Docker Hub Release Tag (For updating release target image)
  id: getDockerHubReleaseTag 
  uses: taye/github-action@v1 # custom action for getting the docker hub release tag created in the previous step to update it's new image
  with:
    tag_type: 'latest' 
- name: Update the Docker Hub Release Target Image (For updating Dockerhub and releasing the changes) 
  uses: taye/github-action@v1.0.4 # custom action for updating a docker hub target tag with it's latest image
  with:  
    owner: kyso 
    repo: kyselda
    token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} 
- name: Create new docker tag
  run: docker tag $DOCKERHUB_REPOSITORY:$TAGNAME $DOCKERHUB_REPOSITORY:${steps.getDockerHubReleaseTag.outputs.latest} # get the latest tag of the release target and create a new tag for it in the docker registry to avoid cache errors during pushing 
- name: Push to Docker Hub 
  uses: docker/build-push-action@v1.0.2
  with:
    repository: "$DOCKERHUB_REPOSITORY" 
    tag_name: '${steps.getDockerHubReleaseTag.outputs.latest}' # tag the image with the new docker hub release target tag  
    target: 'release-target'
